observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue his effort in complying the timely preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements for fair presentation of Cash in Bank account of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. She shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD as required under Section 69 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382, Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) 2nd Edition and Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits must be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts.
We recommended that the Management seek legal action/remedy against the former Municipal Mayor to enforce the refund/payment of the unsettled cash advances.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement the carrying amount of the property, plant and equipment retired from active use, and held for disposal;
Direct the GSO to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and submit it to the Disposal Committee of the Municipality; and
Direct the Disposal Committee of the Municipality to immediately conduct the disposal proceedings to avoid further deterioration of the properties.

We recommended that the Management stop the payment of salaries and benefits of Mr. Almazan as Sectoral Representative in the Sangguniang Bayan of Mallig, Isabela. Further, sectoral representatives should assume their respective Offices in accordance with pertinent laws.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person to submit an updated GAD sex-disaggregated database in order for the Municipality to formulate an updated gender related issues identified and prioritized in the GAD Plan.
We recommended that the Management conduct Community Based Monitoring System for the effective and efficient delivery of necessary services to the constituents.

We recommended that the management expedite the implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the constituents timely benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by promptly implementing the priority development projects as reflected in its duly approved local development plans and establish the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries; (b) MPDC, to continue to initiate close coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant for the proper monitoring of project implementation and the provision of all the necessary information in the Status Report of Government PPAs before submission to the Audit Team; and (c) Municipal Engineer, to maintain requiring timely implementation of PPAs in coordination with the MPDC under their responsibility at all times.

We recommended that the Management strictly enforce collection of all the amounts due to the Municipality. Moreover, strict measures such as imposition of penalties, discontinuance of water supply and other legal means must be imposed upon the concerned delinquent customers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all the members of the Local Finance Committee (LFC), in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), to initiate the updating of the Municipality’s LRC pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and conduct an information campaign to all real property owners within the Municipality with emphasis of granting discounts when paid before due dates and the possibility of losing their property for not paying at all.

We recommended that the Municipality create new ordinances to increase its resources and the Municipal Treasurer to devise a plan to facilitate the collection of taxes to increase the revenue for a more efficient and effective implementation of its development plans, program objectives and priorities.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer rectify the traversal cracks noted on the above-mentioned projects to avoid further damage of the pavement and communicate to the Audit Team upon completion for evaluation purposes.

We recommended that the Municipality undertake the necessary rectification of the deficiencies noted (scaling of concrete pavement and traversal cracks) on the projects mentioned above and submit pictures taken before, during and after the rectifications made. After which, submit a written request for re-evaluation/inspection thereof together with the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) on the rectifications undertaken, otherwise, the corresponding computed value of the deficiencies noted shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer rectify the traversal cracks noted on the above-mentioned projects to avoid further damage of the pavement and communicate to the Audit Team upon completion for evaluation purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer rectify the scaling noted on the above-mentioned project to avoid further damage of the pavement and communicate to the Audit Team upon completion for evaluation purposes.

We recommended that at the close of each business day, the collectors/tellers accomplish the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) in four copies. The original and two copies, together with the duplicate of the official receipts issued, shall be submitted to the treasurer/cashier to whom the cash collected shall be turned over.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day and shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD as required under Section 69 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382, Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) 2nd Edition and Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts.
We recommended that the Management ensure strict compliance with the procedures and guidelines set forth in Item 6.1 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize the collectibles from customers of the Mallig Water System in compliance with IPSASs 1 and 9 and enforce collection thereof.

Therefore, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer submit yearly to the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers, with the amount due and collectibles, which would be the basis of the latter, to set-up the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the accounting period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivables following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We likewise recommended for the recognition of impairment allowance of the dormant accounts and continue to review and analyze dormant accounts, and reclassify the dormant and long-outstanding as non-current assets in order to present the reliable balances of the Receivable accounts.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant apply the Perpetual Inventory Method (PIM) in the recognition of inventories upon purchase;
Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct the actual count of inventories regularly and document the results using the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, for each type of inventory item to record all receipts and issuances of inventories.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain the Report of the Physical Count of Biological Assets (RPCBA) as provided under the Manual and the inventory of breeding stocks and the prevailing market price for each item of biological asset as of reporting date and the Municipal Agriculture Officer to provide the inventory of breeding stocks and other biological assets, if any.

We recommended that all the Department Heads concerned submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2022 AAR findings and recommendations. The information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation and submit the same to the Audit Team for evaluation of the actions taken and status of implementation by the Municipality on the audit recommendations in compliance with Section 97 of the General Provisions of RA 11639. Likewise, ensure thereafter that a status report on the actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations be submitted to COA within sixty (60) days from the date of receipt of AAR.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate a focal person to consolidate the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) from different Offices to facilitate the submission of report to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit at the beginning of the year to the Audit Team the List of all on-going government projects, programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) within the time frame stated in Section 12.2 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 as reiterated in GPPB Resolution No. 11-2020, dated May 20, 2020 for monitoring and compliance with the provisions of the Procurement Law.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus and disclose the same on the face of the Cash Flow Statement or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with Section 29 of IPSAS No. 2.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant properly disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the nature of relationship with related parties involved  in the transactions and description of the related party transactions that transpired for CY 2023 between the Municipality and MADACO/MADAMCO. We further recommended to the Management to further disclose a summary of the broad terms and conditions of transactions with related parties, including disclosure on how these terms and conditions differ from those normally associated with similar transactions with unrelated parties.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible remedies to gather all documents and information needed to determine the details of the account.
We therefore recommended that the Management issue a demand letter to the former Municipal Mayor to liquidate or refund the unsettled cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to:
Furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of contracts and Purchase Orders (PO), irrespective of amount and each of every supporting document, within five working days from the issuance thereof, in compliance with Items 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
Notify the Office of Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of procured items within 24 hours from its acceptance, in compliance with Item A (2) of COA Circular No. 96-010.

We, therefore recommended that Management always ensure the ear tagging of all Breeding Stocks for inventory control purposes and to ensure the proper handling and management thereof. It is likewise recommended the installation of sound internal control in order to prevent loss thru proper identification of Breeding Stocks.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required supporting information on the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS No. 1 for the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO and all his Department Heads in charge observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF; and strictly utilize the funds according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. She shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD as required under Section 69 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382, Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) 2nd Edition and Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, proper handling of collections and its deposits be strictly observed and deposits should be attributed/traced from the collected amounts

Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer commented that due to the laxity and inadvertence on the part of their collectors to remit their daily collections on time and unavailability of government vehicle, the Municipal Treasurer could not deposit their collection within the prescribed period. On the other hand, she mentioned that the Management already called the attention of their staffs who are responsible to remit collections regularly and reminded them that as government employees, they are bound to exercise prudence and due diligence in the performance of task assigned to them. They also commented that the Municipal Mayor guaranteed that government vehicle will be readily available for use in case the Municipal Treasure will deposit her collections.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The audit team will validate the implementation of the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer direct the preparer of checks to be cautious in entering data in the face of the checks and device a control measure to avoid the frequent error and waste of government resources.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the limit of disbursements/payments of expenses out of the Municipal Treasurer’s cash advances only for the purpose it was intended.

Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer commented that they already desisted from using the excess of cash advances as reimbursements. They committed to use only the cash advances for the purpose it was intended.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will closely monitor the implementation of the audit recommendation.

We recommended Management to advise the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to present fairly the balance of Due to National Agencies (NGAs) and Share from Tobacco Excise Tax as at December 31, 2022. We further recommended the Municipal Accountant to explain or justify why there was no amount presented in the Financial Statements despite the recording of the amount received of P51,187,131.50 from the share of tobacco excise for CY 2020 under the account Share from Tobacco Excise Tax for CY 2020.

Management Comment:
The Management commented that the account Due to NGAs was used because the share from tobacco excise tax was received and deposited in the general fund on December 29, 2022. They also commented that it is deemed proper to record it as liability account rather that income since nominal accounts are closed to Government Equity at the end of the year.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team maintained that the share from tobacco excise tax should be recorded as Share from Tobacco Excise Tax in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and not under the account Due to National Government Agencies. As a rejoinder, the Management committed to properly record the affected accounts and to recognize the amount received from the share of tobacco excise tax for CY 2020 as an income on the year it was received by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the subsidiary accounts under the account Due to LGUs noted above such as the following:
to reclassify the subsidiary account Bid document to Trust Liabilities;
the income recorded under the subsidiary ledger account Trust Fund Municipal and collections from OPLAN visa sticker, drying fee, fees paid by the senior citizens, and rental from heavy equipment should be recorded as income by the Municipality and form part under Special Account in the General Fund; and
to reclassify the subsidiary account Performance Bond to Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable under the Trust Liabilities.

Management Comment:
The Management committed to properly reclassify those affected accounts and comply with the audit recommendations.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team requested that a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the affected accounts will be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for validation purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Accountant to transfer the account Due to National Government Agencies from Trust Liabilities to Inter-Agency Payables and submit a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher to the Office of the Auditor.

Management Comment:
The Management committed to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the affected account.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team requested that a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the affected account will be submitted to the Office of the Auditor for validation purposes.

We recommended that the Management properly disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the nature of the related party relationship with related parties involved in the transactions and description of the related party transactions that transpired for CY 2022 between the Municipality and MADACO/MADAMCO. We further recommended to the Management to further disclose a summary of the broad terms and conditions of transactions with related parties, including disclosure on how these terms and conditions differ from those normally associated with similar transactions with unrelated parties.

Management Comment:
The Management commented that the assistance extended by LGU Mallig to MADACO/MADAMCO are not under the control and influence of the Local Finance Committee (LFC). The Work and Financial Plan prepared by the LFC for Tobacco Excise Tax was duly adopted by the Local Sanggunian and approved by the Department of Budget and Management.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team maintained that the assistance extended by LGU Mallig to MADACO/MADAMCO is within the nature of related party transaction. All members of the LFC are also members of MADACO/MADAMCO. Likewise, almost all members of MADACO are regular employees of LGU Mallig. As a rejoinder, the management committed to properly disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the nature of the related party relationship with related parties involved in the transactions that transpired for CY 2022.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management observe the following measures:
advise the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) to update annually the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and
advise the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for the LDRRMF for QRF, MOOE and CO.

We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher on the transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE amounting to ₱3,442,797.06 and submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher.

Management Comment:
The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendation and already prepared an entry to transfer the unutilized LDRRMF from general fund to trust fund.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team requested to the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for validation purposes.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all development projects are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready in order to complete them within the target completion date to maximize the benefit for its intended beneficiaries.

Management Comment:
The Management informed the Audit Team that the Construction of the Sanitary Landfill (Cell 2) and the Soil Covering of Cell 1 were already completed.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will conduct an inspection for validation purposes.

We recommended the management thru the Local Chief Executive the following courses of action:
require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% Development Fund including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target to avoid generic or lumpsum projects; and
stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

Management Comment:
The Management committed to stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sangunian. They also committed to provide specific details on the identified priority projects of the Municipality funded under the 20% Development Fund.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The auditor will closely monitor the implementation of the audit recommendations.

We recommended the following to the Management:
Immediately submit the certified true copy of the Articles of Cooperation and By-Laws of MADACO and MADAMCO to the Office of the Auditor;
Immediately submit the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by and between LGU Mallig and MADACO and/or MADAMCO from CY 2020 to CY 2023 to the Office of the Auditor; and
Explain or justify why the Municipality did not have any record in the books on biological assets considering that the Municipality incurred the amount of ₱13,493,252.79 for CY 2022 for the feeds/foods of biological assets, payment of biological supplies, seedlings, fertilizers, corn silage, dairy cattle pellet and corn seeds that were used in the farm of the Municipality.

Management Comment:
The Management already submitted the required documents to the Office of the Auditor on March 6, 2023.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will further evaluate the documents submitted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise:
the Municipal Accountant to explain the discrepancies noted in the beginning and ending balances of the account Share from Tobacco Excise tax for CYs 2018 and 2019;
the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to explain how they were able to disburse the amount of ₱86,832,434.39 from March 16, 2022 to August 2, 2022 from the share of tobacco excise tax for CY 2019 considering that such share was received on August 3, 2022;
and explain the discrepancy between the cash in bank balance of the 2018 and 2019 share from tobacco excise tax and its ending balance as presented in the Notes to Financial Statement amounting to ₱109,739,528.18.

Management Comment:
Management commented that the share from tobacco excise tax for CY 2019 was received in two (2) tranches. The first tranche was recorded under the Trust Fund Book and second tranche was recorded under the General Fund Book. As such, the amount of ₱86,832,434.39 was disbursed using the trust fund.  The second tranche which was received on August 3, 2022, was recorded under the General Fund Book. On the other hand, they committed to reconcile the discrepancies noted in the audit recommendation.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will closely monitor the implementation of the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipality complete the equivalent deficient amounts for the projects mentioned through work completion and the installation of the 12 pieces of 4” dia. G.I Pipe S#40 amounting to P78,415.61; installation of the Oxidized Asphalt Sealant amounting to P4,350.00; excavation of around 50.32 cubic meter of materials amounting to P13,710.17; and 5 spans with longitudinal crack for corrective measures amounting to P67,652.44 otherwise, the same amount shall be disallowed in audit. We also recommended that the Municipality communicate to the Audit Team the actions taken for evaluation.

Management Comment:
Management commented that they already allocated and implemented the Construction of Slope Protection of the damaged portion of the PCCP of the Trinidad Farm to Market Road Project. LGU Mallig and the contractor (E.S Pua Construction) who implemented the project agreed that the latter will repair the damaged portion of the PCCP at his own expense. The Management also committed to submit a status report upon completion of the repair work.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will closely monitor the implementation of the audit recommendations.

We have reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the LGU create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the Municipality, prepare and submit a reliable RPCPPE thereon. Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records and to instruct the General Services Officer to maintain an accurate, complete and updated property records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management thru the Property/Supply Officer/ LGU Inspector accomplish the pre-repair inspection report after the conduct of pre-repair inspection of properties subjected for repairs and furnished a copy of this report to the COA Auditor within five (5) days from the date of evaluation/inspection. Likewise, when the subject properties were already repaired, conduct immediately post-repair inspection and submit a report thereon. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to require the attachment of the Waste Materials Report in all payments for repairs and maintenance of vehicles and similar nature as part of the supporting documents.

observations.reason: Pre-Repair and Inspection Report and Waste Material Report must be submitted to accounting office as part of documentation for the payment of repair and replacement of equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting of cash donations for DRRM, in lieu of the monthly submission of report on sources and utilization of DRRM fund in Section V-A and B of COA Circular No. 2014-012 dated April 15, 2014.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Require the property and accounting section maintain complete, updated and accurate Property Card and PPE Ledger Card, respectively; and
Require the Municipal Accountant provide a full and appropriate disclosure of the PPE account in the Notes to Financial Statements specifically the road networks; and prepare the necessary entries using the prescribed account titles to properly classify the affected PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Management instruct both the Accounting Section and Property Section to analyze and reconcile their records on a regular basis so that discrepancies thereon are immediately corrected. After reconciling the above discrepancies, inform the Office of the Auditor on the action taken by the Accounting and Property Section.

We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for General Fund and Trust Fund on the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts, in order to present fairly the balance of the affected account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the CY 2021 and succeeding years Notes to Financial Statements the details on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF of the Municipality using the format as required under Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Management:
Refrain from appropriating funds to unrelated projects and activities of disaster risk reduction and management and adhere to the provisions of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and
Explain/ justify why the above mentioned PPAs were funded under the LDRRMF to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance on the amount disbursed for CY 2021.

We recommended that the Management observe the following measures:
Advise the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) update annually the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund and submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF through the LDRRMC and Municipal Development Council on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and
Advise the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for the LDRRMF for QRF, MOOE and CO.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Engineer submit the Variation Orders 1 and 2, Agency Certificate of Acceptance and Test Result, if any, of the contracts/projects enumerated in Table 1;
Submit a written justification on the deficiencies noted in the Construction of Commercial Building; require the Contractor to undertake the unaccomplished items mentioned by our TAS; and submit a written request for re-evaluation/inspection thereof together with the SWA on the rectifications undertaken, otherwise, the corresponding computed value of the deficiencies noted shall be disallowed in audit.; and
Undertake the necessary corrective measures/rectification of the deficiencies noted (scaling of PCCP) and submit pictures taken before, during and after the rectifications made.  After which, submit a written request for re-evaluation/inspection thereof together with the SWA on the rectifications undertaken, otherwise, the corresponding computed value of the deficiencies noted shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Management submit immediately to the Auditor’s Office the disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other supporting documents for the month of December 2021. Likewise, explain/justify the late submission of the said reports. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary reports on timely basis and observe provisions under COA Circular No. 2009-006 and P.D. 1445 in order to avoid sanctions set by the provision.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the MOA or Trust Agreement and the Report on Receipt and Utilization of the above subject PPAs; and
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee submit to the Office of the Auditor the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) and Program of Works of the above subject PPAs.

We recommended that the Management refrain from charging the wages of job order workers and administrative expenses such as supplies and meals and snacks to the 20% DF. However, for CY 2022, we recommended to charge the wages of job orders of greening program to the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) under the General Fund of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management justify/explain why Shopping is the procurement modality for the purchase of office supplies and drugs and medicines considering that the cost of procured goods exceeded the monetary threshold amounting to ₱100,000.00 where shopping maybe used as the mode of procurement. Likewise, we recommended that the Procuring Entity comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Management submit immediately the complete documentary requirements, as stated above, on the payment of honoraria to the members of the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group to avoid the suspension of the transactions. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop reimbursement of claims without complete document.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer should discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation should always be observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations. Likewise, officials who continue to authorize the payment of such expenditures are jointly and severally liable for any disallowances.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit proof of community meetings organized by the Project Facilitator and the registration of unemployed or underemployed members of the community who shall be as the labor source pakyaw group.
Avoid awarding of pakyaw contracts to the same pakyaw labor group for two or more different pakyaw labor works having overlapping period of implementation;
Submit Certificate of Pakyaw Work Completed issued by the Pakyaw Group and Certificate of Acceptance for Pakyaw Work Completed for all infrastructure projects implemented and completed employing the Pakyaw Work System; and
Strictly comply with the provision of Sec. 4(6) and Sec. 88 of P.D. 1445 and Item 4.0 of Annex A of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 dated December 6, 2006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, adopt and implement guidelines taking into consideration the following:
Review the amounts of mobile telephone allowances in relation to the available postpaid lines which are in reasonable amounts from P599.00 to P999.00 per month for heads of offices/elected officials taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication services providers. At any rate, any excess on the limit set, shall be for the personal account of the user; and
Utilize alternatives modes of communication such as internet and Facebook for long-distance communication or consider the features of communication network of its free long-distance calls for Smart to Smart/Globe to Globe phones within the region that provides cost savings measures.

We recommended that the LGU management terminate the designation of the Municipal Treasurer as member of the BAC-TWG and strictly observe/implement BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 015-2019 dated 27 June.

We recommended that the Management observe the following measures:
Advise the Human Resource Management Officer ensure that the net take home pay of employees for FY 2022 shall not be less than the minimum allowed net take home pay of five thousand pesos (₱5,000.00) as provided in Section 55 of R.A No. 11639, GAA for FY 2022; and
Refrain from being a withholding agent for loan obligations of employees in institutions not allowed in the provisions of the applicable GAA and hold the withholding agent responsible for allowing the net take home pay of the employees lesser than that allowed by the GAA.

We recommended that the Management thru the Municipal Treasurer monitor and strictly require the Revenue Collectors to remit/turnover all their daily collections within the same day at the close of office hours in order that their daily collections shall be deposited promptly to the depository bank of the Municipality in accordance with Section 63 and 66 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs and R.A 7160 and to strictly observe the regulation in the ensuing year.

The Municipal Treasurer should set control measures to avoid or minimize delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action to concerned accountable employees under her supervision, if necessary.

Concerned officers and employees immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, their salaries and other benefits shall be withheld and applied to their outstanding cash advances.

The Municipal Accountant issue a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting additional cash advances.

The Municipal Accountant should be careful in choosing account titles to be used in their Financial Statement. He should follow the account titles prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-09.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require all officials and employees with unliquidated Cash Advances to submit the liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books and refund the excess balances, if any, to fully settle their outstanding accounts, and 
 
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the liquidation of Cash Advances granted to Accountable Officers within the prescribed period to ensure prompt settlement of accounts. The “no liquidation no cash advance policy” must be strictly enforced by the Management.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a demand letter to Malaya Development Cooperative for the immediate settlement/payment of their outstanding loan balance of P1,400,000.00 to the Department of Agriculture thru the LGU of Mallig.

We recommended the following to the Management:
The Municipal Engineer conduct physical inventory of all road networks under the jurisdiction of the Municipality using the road map as basis, and provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete inventory report using the form provided under Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
The General Services Officer maintains a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (Annex A of COA Circular No. 2015-008).
The Municipal Accountant prepares the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.
The Municipal Engineer, General Service Officer and the Municipal Accountant coordinate to update reporting and accounting guidelines for the local road network.

We recommended that the Management prepare an updated physical inventory report of all its properties. We also recommended that the General Services Officer/Property Custodian maintain property records.

We recommended that the GSO consolidate the PPMP into APP to arrive to a quarterly procurement of office supplies procured thru public bidding and comply strictly with the mandate of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Stop withdrawing cash more than the amount needed for a specific purpose.
Account for all undeposited collections and explain failure to deposit the collections intact and within the prescribed periods; and
Deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that:
Management strictly follow the rules in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002.
Concerned officers and employees immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances.
The Municipal Accountant issue a certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting additional cash advances.
The Municipal Accountant should be careful in choosing account titles to be used in their Financial Statement. He should follow the account titles prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-09.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a demand letter to Malaya Development Cooperative for the immediate settlement/payment of their outstanding loan balance of P1,400,000.00 to the Department of Agriculture thru the LGU of Mallig.

We recommended that the Management comply strictly wit COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 94-006 such as:
Furnish the Auditor copy of contract (including purchase orders and memorandum agreement) and each off all the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation within five (5) working days from the execution of a contracts; and
Notify the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours from accepting deliveries of procured items.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to prepare the necessary pre-repair and post-repair inspection reports for all repairs and maintenance of service vehicle and forward to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the same within 5 days after the conduct of the inspection to give enough time for the Auditor to evaluate said report.

Record the transfer of funds under the inter-agency receivables account Due from LGUs and Due from NGAs in order to establish accountability and responsibility and to ensure that the transfer is properly taken up in the books of both agencies.
Establish specific guidelines and policies in the grant of all kinds of financial assistance.

We recommended that the Management ensure that contracts of service of all hired Job Order Worker are properly signed both by the Local Chief Executive and the hired Job Order Worker before allowing the latter to start their work and payments for the services rendered. Furthermore, copy of the said documents should be attached to the claim in order to support its legality and validity.

We recommended that payments for supplies and materials as well as other operating expenses be made directly to the suppliers. Limit payments through reimbursement and deduct/withhold the mandatory taxes. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 93 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Budget Officer appropriate one percent (1%) of IRA for LCPC programs, projects and activities for the protection of children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Management monitor and ensure the full implementation and completion of all the LGUs programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.
We commended the agency, particularly the Municipal Treasurer and Staff in exerting extra-effort to increase its collections, with recommendation of continuing a job well done.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit regularly and intact the full amount in accordance with Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 111 of GAAM. Likewise, stop the practice of leaving huge amount of cash in her possession to avoid possible loss through theft or robbery, misappropriation or misapplication of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory and to reconcile the inventory records against the accounting records.

Include the cost of tarpaulin in the preparation of the Program of Work of all infrastructure projects.
